title: Configure a personal Code Owner review boundary
outOfScope: Claiming two independent maintainers when only one personal Code Owner is configured
Production release publication or enterprise trust claims

sources.reason: Task 8 acceptance and human-gated review boundary.
Repository-controlled approval versus trusted platform identity boundary.
Existing fail-closed readiness diagnostic for CODEOWNERS placeholders.
Readiness regression tests for external ownership configuration.
Platform evidence for required approvals, Code Owner review, stale review dismissal, and conversation resolution.

problemStatement: The template repository had a placeholder personal Code Owner. The requested simplified boundary uses RayIori for template maintenance, while installed target projects must replace that identity and enforce at least one required approval.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: CODEOWNERS names RayIori as the repository Code Owner.
Adopter configuration requires at least one target-project approval after replacing the template owner.
Documentation distinguishes personal Code Owner enforcement from organization Team and independent-maintainer assurance.
Release immutability and external trust claims remain limited without signed tags or independent attestations.

riskAssessment.reason: A personal Code Owner improves review enforcement but does not provide independent multi-maintainer or organization-Team assurance.

executionDecision.reason: The user explicitly authorized the simplified RayIori personal Code Owner boundary and GitHub Admin access is active.

preReviewWarnings: Do not claim organization-Team review or two-maintainer independence.
Platform evidence must be captured from the active RayIori Admin identity.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record the review-boundary evidence before and after the platform configuration change.

acceptance: CODEOWNERS assigns the governed repository paths to RayIori as a personal Code Owner.
Adopter documentation requires at least one target-project approval after replacing the template owner.
Documentation explicitly limits this simplified boundary and does not claim organization-Team or two-maintainer assurance.
Release immutability claims are limited unless signed tags or independent attestations are actually configured.
guidelines: Treat platform review settings as external evidence, not as Contract self-declarations.
Keep the simplified personal-owner boundary distinct from trusted organization review.
Do not claim release immutability beyond the verified tag and attestation evidence.

intent.problem: The repository needs an enforceable review owner without inventing a nonexistent organization Team.
constraints: Use RayIori as a personal Code Owner.
Do not overclaim two-maintainer or organization-Team assurance.
intent.rationale: A simple personal-owner boundary is immediately actionable while preserving an explicit path to stronger organization review later.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized RayIori personal Code Owner configuration for the template and the adopter approval requirement.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore CODEOWNERS, documentation, tests, and generated cockpit status.
